message:
feat(batching,trace): implement switch/cond batching and trace sub-jaxpr propagation

The batching transform and trace infrastructure now handle equations
with sub-jaxprs, enabling vmap over programs containing switch/cond
control flow. Previously these would fail with unsupported-primitive
errors during batching or lose sub-jaxpr structure during tracing.

- fj-dispatch/batching: add scalar_to_i64, batch_size_from_inputs,
  broadcast_unbatched_outputs, and select_switch_branch helpers;
  implement batch_switch that evaluates the selected branch with
  batched inputs and broadcasts unbatched outputs to match batch size;
  wire into the main batch_equation dispatch for Primitive::Switch
- fj-trace: add sub_jaxprs field to TraceEquation; add
  process_control_flow_primitive() for recording equations that carry
  sub-jaxprs (switch, cond) with automatic num_branches param
  insertion; propagate sub_jaxprs through to closed Jaxpr output
- fj-conformance: expand control_flow_conformance tests with batched
  switch scenarios and multi-branch vmap coverage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -297,6 +297,26 @@ fn switch_branch_self_binary_jaxpr(primitive: Primitive) -> Jaxpr {
     )
 }
 
+fn switch_control_flow_jaxpr() -> Jaxpr {
+    Jaxpr::new(
+        vec![VarId(1), VarId(2)],
+        vec![],
+        vec![VarId(3)],
+        vec![Equation {
+            primitive: Primitive::Switch,
+            inputs: smallvec::smallvec![Atom::Var(VarId(1)), Atom::Var(VarId(2))],
+            outputs: smallvec::smallvec![VarId(3)],
+            params: BTreeMap::from([("num_branches".to_owned(), "3".to_owned())]),
+            effects: vec![],
+            sub_jaxprs: vec![
+                switch_branch_identity_jaxpr(),
+                switch_branch_self_binary_jaxpr(Primitive::Add),
+                switch_branch_self_binary_jaxpr(Primitive::Mul),
+            ],
+        }],
+    )
+}
+

@@ -608,6 +606,52 @@ fn test_switch_dispatch() {
     }
 }
 
+#[test]
+fn test_vmap_switch_dispatch_with_batched_indices() {
+    let response = dispatch(make_request(
+        switch_control_flow_jaxpr(),
+        vec![
+            Value::vector_i64(&[0, 1, 2]).expect("branch index vector should build"),
+            Value::vector_i64(&[5, 6, 7]).expect("operand vector should build"),
+        ],
+        &[Transform::Vmap],
+        BTreeMap::new(),
+    ))
+    .expect("vmap(switch) dispatch should succeed for batched indices");
+    let result = response.outputs[0]
+        .as_tensor()
+        .expect("vmap(switch) output should be a tensor");
+    let values: Vec<i64> = result
+        .elements
+        .iter()
+        .map(|lit| lit.as_i64().expect("i64 element"))
+        .collect();
+    assert_eq!(values, vec![5, 12, 49]);
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn test_vmap_switch_dispatch_with_scalar_index_and_batched_operand() {
+    let response = dispatch(make_request(
+        switch_control_flow_jaxpr(),
+        vec![
+            Value::scalar_i64(1),
+            Value::vector_i64(&[2, 3, 4]).expect("operand vector should build"),
+        ],
+        &[Transform::Vmap],
+        BTreeMap::from([("vmap_in_axes".to_owned(), "none,0".to_owned())]),
+    ))
+    .expect("vmap(switch) dispatch should batch the selected branch for a scalar index");
+    let result = response.outputs[0]
+        .as_tensor()
+        .expect("vmap(switch) output should be a tensor");
+    let values: Vec<i64> = result
+        .elements
+        .iter()
+        .map(|lit| lit.as_i64().expect("i64 element"))
+        .collect();
+    assert_eq!(values, vec![4, 6, 8]);
+}
+
